parts.name: Part 1
parts.instructions: You must answer this question. Write your answer in 220-260 words in an appropriate style.

tasks.lead: Your class has attended a panel discussion about how museums could remain relevant in the twenty-first century. You have made the notes below. Write an essay discussing two of the ideas in your notes. You should explain which idea is more important, giving reasons to support your opinion.

stimulus.title: How could museums remain relevant in the twenty-first century?
stimulus.body:
• using interactive and digital technology in exhibitions
• involving local communities in deciding what is displayed
• making entry free for everyone

Some opinions expressed in the discussion:
"Glass cases and long labels belong to another century."
"A museum that ignores the people around it deserves to be empty."
"Charging for entry tells half the city the museum is not for them."

bullets: give reasons to support your opinion

parts.name: Part 2
parts.instructions: Answer one of these questions. Write your answer in 220-260 words in an appropriate style.

tasks.lead: The director of your local museum has invited proposals from young visitors.

stimulus.title: Proposal: attracting visitors under 25
stimulus.body: Write a proposal suggesting how the museum could attract more visitors under the age of 25. Explain why young people currently stay away, recommend two specific initiatives, and describe how the museum could measure whether they are working.

bullets: why young people currently stay away
how the museum could measure success

tasks.lead: An English-language culture website has invited reviews of exhibitions.

stimulus.title: Exhibition reviews wanted
stimulus.body: Send us a review of an exhibition you have visited recently. Describe what the exhibition set out to do, evaluate how imaginatively it presented its subject, and say whether it justified the time and money a visitor would spend on it.

bullets: what the exhibition set out to do
how imaginatively it presented its subject
whether it justified the time and money

tasks.lead: You have received this email from your English-speaking friend Jordan.

stimulus.body:
From: Jordan
Subject: Culture trip

I'm finally visiting your country in the spring, and I want to spend a couple of days on museums and galleries - but I refuse to trudge round somewhere boring out of a sense of duty!

Which places would genuinely be worth my time, and is there anything overrated I should skip? Also, any tricks for avoiding the crowds?

Thanks,
Jordan

bullets: which museums or galleries are genuinely worth visiting, and why
